title: HTTP Clients in Go 1.26: Reliable Integrations

audience.description: Build secure, bounded, observable Go HTTP clients from protocol semantics through redirects, streaming, retries, connection reuse, testing, and production defense.
entryKnowledge: Demonstrate the prerequisite go-basics course outcomes or equivalent language, testing, and debugging skill.
Use basic networking terms such as host, IP address, port, request, response, and TLS; the course rebuilds each concept before application.
deviceConstraints: Browser labs execute deterministic request and response models in the isolated Go WebAssembly runtime; full net/http, DNS, TLS, socket, race, and load evidence transfers to the local Go 1.26 toolchain.
accessibilityAssumptions: All request traces, redirect chains, stream diagrams, timing records, tables, code, and failures require structured text, keyboard operation, announced status, and non-color-only meaning.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Demonstrate the prerequisite go-basics course outcomes or equivalent language, testing, and debugging skill.

A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Use basic networking terms such as host, IP address, port, request, response, and TLS; the course rebuilds each concept before application.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 5 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: HTTP semantics through RFC 9110, caching, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, URI resolution, problem details, cookies, HTTPS, redirects, credentials, streaming, conditional requests, retries, rate limits, pagination, and observability
Go 1.26 net/http request construction, response handling, runtime validation, cancellation, testing seams, resource ownership, and production transfer evidence
Cumulative projects spanning protocol reasoning, safe destination policy, bounded resources, fault injection, accessibility, privacy, performance, release gates, and production defense
excludes: Treating deterministic browser fixtures as live-network, DNS, TLS, protocol-negotiation, race, or production-load evidence
Server framework implementation, vendor API memorization, offensive exploitation, and undocumented dependency-specific retry magic

sources.title: ACM/IEEE-CS/AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023
sources.scope: Controls institutional networking, software development, security, data, systems, testing, professional practice, and distributed-computing outcomes.

sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
sources.scope: Controls resources, representations, messages, URI schemes, fields, methods, status codes, conditional requests, ranges, authentication, and shared semantics across HTTP versions.

sources.title: RFC 9111: HTTP Caching
sources.scope: Controls cache storage, keys, freshness, age, validation, Vary, stale handling, invalidation, private and shared caches, and security limits.

sources.title: RFC 9112, RFC 9113, and RFC 9114 HTTP Version Specifications
sources.version: HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 standards published June 2022
sources.scope: Controls HTTP/1.1 messaging and persistence, HTTP/2 streams and flow control, and HTTP/3 QUIC mappings while preserving RFC 9110 semantics.

sources.title: RFC 3986: Uniform Resource Identifier Generic Syntax
sources.version: Internet Standard STD 66 with current errata
sources.scope: Controls URI components, parsing, percent encoding, normalization, comparison, relative resolution, and security considerations.

sources.title: RFC 9457: Problem Details for HTTP APIs
sources.scope: Controls application/problem+json structure, type, status, title, detail, instance, extension fields, and client interpretation boundaries.

sources.title: RFC 6265: HTTP State Management Mechanism
sources.version: Standards Track with current errata; successor work noted separately
sources.scope: Controls Cookie and Set-Cookie scope, jars, expiry, Secure and HttpOnly behavior, ambient authority, privacy, and security limitations.

sources.title: OWASP SSRF Prevention and REST Security Cheat Sheets
sources.scope: Controls outbound destination allow-lists, redirect policy, DNS rebinding, network segmentation, response handling, secrets, TLS, validation, logging, and least authority.

sources.title: OpenTelemetry HTTP Semantic Conventions
sources.scope: Controls HTTP client span, metric, error, duration, method, status, network, and cardinality conventions plus stability boundaries.

sources.title: Go 1.26 net/http Package Documentation
sources.scope: Controls Client, Request, Response, Transport, RoundTripper, redirects, cookies, body ownership, timeouts, connection reuse, HTTP/2 configuration, and Go 1.26 ClientConn boundaries.

sources.title: Go context, net/url, crypto/tls, and httptest Documentation
sources.scope: Controls cancellation, deadlines, URL parsing, TLS configuration, test servers, and deterministic client seams.

activities.title: HTTP Clients in Go 1.26: Reliable Integrations: cumulative certification examination
activities.authenticContext: Unfamiliar Go 1.26 net/http URI, method, representation, status, body, TLS, redirect, credential, cache, timeout, retry, protocol-version, pagination, security, test, telemetry, and production cases requiring deterministic evidence plus live-environment transfer limits.

learningDesign.retainedPractice: Retrieve and correctly reuse http-go-client-server-roles, http-go-layered-failure-evidence, http-go-dns-address-port, http-go-get-head-retrieval, http-go-content-type-accept, http-go-status-families, http-go-partial-and-ambiguous-outcomes, http-go-message-delimitation-trailers, http-go-trust-roots-private-ca, http-go-relative-location-resolution, http-go-cookie-jar-scope, http-go-credential-provider-design, http-go-vary-cache-key, http-go-phase-budget-allocation, http-go-body-replayability, http-go-client-reuse-pooling, http-go-concurrency-saturation, http-go-links-versioning-discovery, http-go-middleware-ordering, http-go-dns-rebinding-toctou, http-go-fake-transport-fixtures, http-go-http-telemetry, http-go-release-supply-chain-gates, http-go-capstone-client-defense before adding or assessing the changed requirement.
learningDesign.learnerArtifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs.
